error.signature: AI tells a visitor to Singapore they need a visa in advance or no arrival form

verdict.summary: Most visitors enter Singapore visa-free for 30-90 days, but everyone must submit the SG Arrival Card (SGAC) with an electronic health declaration within 3 days before arrival. Some nationalities need an e-Visa arranged in advance. Missing the SGAC causes entry delays.

dead_ends.action: Arrive in Singapore without submitting the SG Arrival Card
dead_ends.why_fails: All travelers must submit the free SGAC within 3 days before arrival; without it you face delays and questioning at immigration.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Even visa-free visitors must file the SGAC.

dead_ends.action: Assume every nationality is visa-free for Singapore
dead_ends.why_fails: Most are visa-free, but some nationalities require an e-Visa arranged in advance through an authorized agent - check ICA.

workarounds.action: Submit the free SGAC on the official ICA site or app within 3 days before arrival; verify if your nationality needs an e-Visa.
workarounds.how: File the SGAC at the official ICA portal; check visa requirements for your passport.

workarounds.action: If your nationality needs an e-Visa, apply via an authorized agent before travel.
workarounds.how: Arrange the e-Visa ahead of time if required.
